w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: Novel Spatial Structure for the future, The Design and Research of New Industrialized Green Energy-saving Building System,

Developed a multimodal emotion-aware VR dialogue system for mental health intervention, integrating speech and text emotion analysis. Investigated the effectiveness of empathy-based AI responses in VR therapeutic settings.
Implemented VR dialogue system using Unity and ONNX
, created prompts leveraging VAD emotion models, and designed emotionally adaptive avatars. Focused on core architecture and AI response calibration.
Research Intern, Narrative Space Lab, City University of Hong Kong
Developed a multi-agent-powered narrative game to explore how group dynamics in virtual communities influence users
sustainability attitudes and decision-making. The game used real-time environmental feedback to simulate collective persuasion and its effects on value alignment.

Designed multi-agent dialogue systems using prompt engineering and built visual assets representing differing social stances (supportive, opposing, neutral), enabling complex, emotionally responsive interactions.

Investigated the potential effectiveness of AI-driven VR therapy for Social Anxiety Disorder (SAD), comparing outcomes with traditional CBT through a qualitative systematic review and prototype simulation.
Built the Unity-based VR simulation, performed data analysis using GAD-7 and STAI-S metrics, and visualized comparative results between AI+VR and CBT interventions.
Evaluated how virtual natural environments affect psychological recovery among students using physiological data (e.g., HR, BVP) in immersive VR settings.
Constructed VR scenes in Unity using HTC
, collected biometric data with E4 wristbands, and conducted statistical analyses to propose design guidelines for restorative academic VR environments.
Analyzed the restorative psychological effects of varying indoor green space designs in university environments using visual greenery metrics and recovery scales.
Modeled different indoor planting configurations, calculated Green View Index (GLR), and ran statistical tests (reliability analysis, ANOVA, t-tests) using SPSS to identify effective design patterns.
Designing Constrained Generative NPCs with a Dynamic Evidence Graph for Investigative Narrative Worlds
. In Extended Abstracts of the 2026 CHI Conference on Human Factors in Computing Systems (CHI EA '26). ACM.
Zhao, Z. (2026). Multimodal Emotion Perception for VR Dialogue: Towards Improved User Experience. In
Proceedings of the IEEE VR 2026 Conference on Virtual Reality and 3D User Interfaces
Zhao, Z., & Yin, C. (2025, July). Leveraging AI‐driven VR environments to reshape urban planning and community engagement. Paper presented at the
19th International Conference on Computational Urban Planning and Urban Management (CUPUM 2025)
Yin, C., Li, T., Xue, B., & Zhao, Z. (2025). Influence of virtual reality restorative environments on psychological well-being in university students: An evidence-based experimental study.

Worked on an immersive VR museum in Unreal Engine to digitally reconstruct over 200 Brazilian Indigenous artifacts. I was responsible for 3D modeling, interactive narrative logic, and ensuring cultural accuracy in collaboration with anthropologists.

Worked on an immersive VR museum in Unreal Engine to digitally reconstruct over 200 Brazilian Indigenous artifacts. I was responsible for 3D modeling, interactive narrative logic, and ensuring cultural accuracy in collaboration with anthropologists.

Contributed to the early-phase urban planning of mixed-use complexes. My work included site analysis, zoning diagrams, Rhino-based volumetric studies, and assisting with technical design documentation
Architectural Design Institute Co., Ltd., Xinjiang
Assisted in concept and façade design using Rhino + Grasshopper. Participated in performance-driven modeling, drawing revisions, and interdisciplinary coordination for project optimization.
Digital Heritage – Nanjing Da Bao En Temple
Led the design of an AR/VR heritage experience reconstructing a UNESCO site with interactive storytelling and AI dialogues for a global competition.
Developed a symbolic underwater VR film in Unreal blending mythology with ecological critique, presented in virtual art showcases.
The project was also featured at the
"Shanghai Three Days · Future: To the Future"
Created an AR eco-game featuring magical plants and interactive feedback loops to enhance environmental awareness through playful mechanics.
LLM Prompt Design, Multi-Agent Dialogue, Emotion-Adaptive Systems, Conversational Memory
